most_recent_enforcement_milestone.description: Governance chapter and rules for General-Purpose AI (GPAI) — including AI Office powers, penalties framework, and market-surveillance/notified-body provisions — became applicable.

primary_source.title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (Artificial Intelligence Act) — EUR-Lex
primary_source.note: See timeline and application dates in the AI Act text (OJ / EUR-Lex).

obligations_to_prepare_for.obligation: data governance and data quality requirements
conformity assessment and CE-marking where applicable

parallel_current_obligations.description: GPAI-specific governance duties and the voluntary General-Purpose AI Code (published 2025-07-10)

uncertainties_and_timing_ambiguities: Classification of high-risk systems (Article 6) — Commission-level guidance and examples had a statutory deadline (no later than 2026-02-02) but publication was delayed; lack of clear examples creates practical legal uncertainty for teams deciding whether Chapter III applies.

Availability of harmonised standards and notified-body capacity — standards development and notified-body readiness lag in implementation trackers, affecting conformity-assessment practicality and timing.

Member-state designation and enforcement variance — national competent authorities and market-surveillance resources vary by Member State, which may lead to uneven enforcement and interpretive differences.

Interaction with sectoral rules and GDPR — many AI products also fall under product-safety, medical-device, automotive, or data-protection regimes; combined compliance paths can be complex and timing-sensitive.
GPAI regime nuance — rules for GPAI are in force but the Code of Practice is voluntary; enforcement practices and transition windows for existing vs new models have nuance that teams must track.
recommended_immediate_actions_for_produc: Run a classification readiness review and document rationale for (non-)high-risk determinations (keep Article-6 documentation).

If potentially high-risk: start/strengthen a risk-management system, prepare Annex IV technical documentation, implement logging and post-market monitoring, map data governance and retention, and identify conformity-assessment route early.
If using or embedding GPAI: review the General-Purpose AI Code of Practice (EC, 2025-07-10) and engage with AI Office resources.

Track three sources regularly: EUR-Lex (AI Act consolidated text), European Commission / AI Office publications (guidelines, Codes, templates), and national competent-authority notices for markets of operation.

sources.title: European Commission press release: General-Purpose AI Code of Practice
AI Act Service Desk — Article 6 (High-risk classification) page
Industry/legal analyses on standards and notified-body readiness (example: Taylor Wessing)
Independent reporting on delayed Article-6 guidance (example: Hyperight)
